description_long: This object is a single, elegant red rose with a long, slender green stem and two sets of green leaves attached at different heights. The rose bloom is composed of multiple overlapping, velvety petals arranged in a tight, spiral whorl, creating a classic, full appearance. The petals are deep red, with subtle variations in hue, and the edges are gently curved, giving the flower a lifelike softness. The stem is straight and smooth, tapering slightly toward the base, and the leaves are ovate with visible midribs and a matte finish. The flower sits at the terminal end of the stem, standing alone without any visible thorns. This rose would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative arrangements along sidewalks or public spaces.
description: A single red rose with a spiral arrangement of velvety petals, a long green stem, and two sets of ovate green leaves. The flower is full and lifelike, ideal for urban decorative settings.
description_view_0: The rose is seen from the front, showing the full bloom and two leaves on the left side of the stem.
description_view_1: The rose is viewed from the side, with both sets of leaves aligned vertically along the stem.
description_view_2: The rose is seen from the back, displaying the bloom and two leaves on the right side of the stem.
description_view_3: The rose is viewed from the opposite side, with both sets of leaves visible but staggered along the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral whorl, overlapping each other in concentric layers, creating a radial symmetry typical of Rosa species. The arrangement is dense and layered, with the outer petals curving outward and the inner petals tightly packed.
